title: TrustForge Policy Manifest
description: Declarative policy definition referenced by TF-0004. Backend-agnostic (Cedar, Rego, custom, native, none).

policy_version.description: Version of the policy manifest schema itself.

trust_domain.description: Trust domain this policy applies within.

engine_hint.description: Policy engine that interprets this manifest.

rules.description: Policy rules evaluated top-to-bottom until a match yields a decision.

negative_capabilities.description: Explicit denials that override grants regardless of rule order.

quorum_defaults.description: Default quorum settings when a rule requests quorum approval without specifying one.

continuous_reevaluation.description: When live sessions must re-check this policy during execution.

triggers.description: Events that force a re-evaluation of in-flight authorizations.

id.description: Rule identifier, used in proofs and audit logs.

effect.description: Decision produced when the rule matches.

action.description: Exact action this rule applies to.

action_pattern.description: Regex (ECMAScript) matched against action names when an exact action is not set.

subject_pattern.description: Regex matched against the subject actor URI.

target_patterns.description: Glob patterns matched against the action target.

risk_at_most.description: Rule applies only to actions whose risk is at or below this class.

proof_required.description: Minimum proof level demanded when this rule applies.

approval.description: Approval requirement demanded when this rule applies.

constraints.description: Additional constraints attached by this rule.

reason.description: Human-readable reason emitted in the decision.
